title: Optimizer should invalidate artifact reuse when feedback target mutates

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Investigation summary: fresh feedback selectors now exclude isInvalid=true in Evaluation._fetch_feedback_items and FeedbackService.find_feedback_items, and associated dataset curation rejects invalid items. Remaining leak is artifact reuse in feedback_alignment_optimizer.yaml: existing associated dataset is reused by row_count/materialization only; evaluation_find_recent cache reuse is keyed too loosely (accuracy keyed by score_version only, feedback keyed by score_version/type/age only) and ignores feedback target mutations after invalidation/edit. Planned fix: add feedback updatedAt watermark tool, tighten dataset+baseline cache matching, and stop runs on mid-run feedback drift.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented fix set:
1) Added MCP tool plexus_feedback_latest_update in MCP/tools/feedback/feedback.py. It computes latest relevant FeedbackItem.updatedAt watermark while restricting candidates to the score window by editedAt+days.
2) Tightened plexus_evaluation_find_recent in MCP/tools/evaluation/evaluations.py with optional exact-match filters (dataset_id, days, max_feedback_items, sampling_mode) plus latest_feedback_updated_at freshness gate for feedback baselines.
3) Updated optimizer procedure wiring in plexus/procedures/feedback_alignment_optimizer.yaml:
   - computes/stores feedback_target_updated_at watermark
   - rejects associated dataset reuse when dataset.created_at < watermark
   - passes exact-match arguments to plexus_evaluation_find_recent for both accuracy and feedback baseline reuse
   - checks watermark at cycle start and stops run with stop_reason=feedback_target_changed_restart_required when drift detected
4) Updated skills/plexus-score-optimizer/SKILL.md with artifact-reuse freshness/restart policy.
5) Added tests:
   - MCP/tools/feedback/feedback_latest_update_test.py
   - MCP/tools/evaluation/evaluation_find_recent_exact_match_test.py
Validation:
- Syntax sanity passed via python3 -m py_compile for modified Python files.
- Could not execute pytest in current shell: pytest is not installed on available interpreters in this environment.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Validation update: executed syntax check successfully via python3 -m py_compile on modified Python files. Attempted targeted pytest for new tests, but local system interpreter environment lacks MCP runtime deps ( import fails in this shell), so runtime unit execution could not be completed locally. Tests are added and ready for execution in the project runtime/CI environment where MCP deps are available.
